matches.snippet: orange fin with a mosaic of dispensations and a froren black patata
matches.reasoning: Pun on 'fin' sounding like 'Finn'; in context of enumeration of gifts/adornments at ceremonial gathering, evokes Finnegan as the central figure being honored

matches.snippet: He cawls to me Granny-stream-Auborne when I am hiding under my hair from him and I cool him my Finnyking he's so joyant a bounder.
matches.reasoning: Direct avatar epithet for the hero; 'Finnyking' is a term of endearment/epithet applied to the beloved figure, fusing Finn with royalty/kingship

matches.snippet: I have it here to my fingall's ends. This liggy piggy wanted to go to the jampot.
matches.reasoning: Compound pun: 'fingall' evokes both 'fingernail' and 'Fin' + 'gall'; 'gall' recalls the stone/gall of Finnegan's Wake ballad. Wordplay suggests Finn's tactile/physical presence ('to my fingall's ends' = within my grasp/control, Finn's reach)

matches.snippet: But there's leps of flam in Funnycoon's Wick. The keyn has passed. Lung lift the keying!
matches.reasoning: Pun on 'Funny' (Finn) disguised in proper noun 'Funnycoon's'; 'Wick' (dwelling/place) suggests Finnegan's locale. Plays on the phonetic proximity to 'Funny' / 'Finn' while obscuring direct reference

Direct garbled spelling variants of Finn: 'Finnk' (Finn + k), 'Fime' (Finn variant with 'me'), 'Fudd' (Finn variant with 'dd'). Part of enumeration/listing of names, suggesting Finnegan in corrupted form

matches.snippet: Dovegall and finshark, they are ring to the rescune!
matches.reasoning: Direct spelling 'finshark' contains 'fin' in compound; unclear meaning intensifies the Joycean obfuscation. 'Fin' + 'shark' may evoke predatory/mythic power (Salmon of Knowledge association?)
